summary: Install-time credential harvester exfiltrating SSH keys and npm tokens to hardcoded bare-IP C2
details: On npm install, postinstall.js reads installer-side credentials — /root/.ssh/id_rsa.pub, contents of /root/.npm/_logs, and any strings matching `_authToken=` under /root/.npm — then base64-wraps the captured data and PUTs it via http.request to http://154.57.164.64:32271/api/modules/ECT-839201 and /ECT-472839. The destination is a bare IPv4 address over plain HTTP, unrelated to any legitimate publisher infrastructure. A gating function (shouldRun) suppresses execution under CI, GitHub Actions, common mirrors, and /tmp working directories, and only fires when the hostname matches a 12-hex-character Docker container ID pattern and the working directory contains /app — a sandbox-evasion shape consistent with targeting a specific containerized environment. The combination of installer credential reads, hardcoded bare-IP exfiltration over cleartext HTTP at postinstall time, and evasion gating is an unambiguous credential-harvest dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
